questions.q: In what year did baseball become America's national pastime?
questions.explanation: Baseball became established as America's national pastime in the late 1800s, with professional organized baseball beginning in 1869 with the Cincinnati Red Stockings. The sport grew rapidly through the early 20th century.

questions.q: Which country invented modern tennis in the 19th century?
questions.explanation: Modern lawn tennis was invented in England in the 1870s by Major Walter Clopton Wingfield. He adapted the ancient game of real tennis for outdoor grass courts, creating the sport we know today.

questions.q: What is the oldest sport still played in its original form?
questions.explanation: Wrestling is considered one of the oldest sports, with evidence dating back to ancient civilizations. It was part of the ancient Olympic Games and remains largely unchanged in its fundamental nature.

questions.q: In what century did golf originate in Scotland?
questions.explanation: Golf originated in Scotland in the 15th century, with evidence suggesting the sport was played around St Andrews. It evolved from earlier games and became formalized with the establishment of golf clubs and rules.

questions.q: Which sport was invented by Dr. James Naismith?
questions.explanation: Basketball was invented by Dr. James Naismith, a Canadian physical educator, in 1891. He created the game as an indoor winter sport using a peach basket as the goal.

questions.q: In what year did the first modern Olympics take place?
questions.explanation: The first modern Olympic Games were held in 1896 in Athens, Greece, reviving the ancient Olympic tradition. Baron Pierre de Coubertin was the driving force behind bringing back the Olympic Games in the modern era.

questions.q: Which legendary boxer was known for his quote 'Float like a butterfly, sting like a bee'?
questions.explanation: Muhammad Ali (formerly Cassius Clay) used the quote 'Float like a butterfly, sting like a bee' to describe his boxing style. Ali is widely considered one of the greatest boxers of all time.

questions.q: What sport did Baron Pierre de Coubertin revive in the modern era?
questions.explanation: Baron Pierre de Coubertin, a French educator and historian, was instrumental in reviving the Olympic Games in the modern era, leading to the first modern Olympics in 1896. He is considered the father of the modern Olympic movement.

questions.q: In what year did Pelé score his 1,000th goal?
questions.explanation: Pelé, the legendary Brazilian footballer, scored his 1,000th goal on November 19, 1969, during a match at the Maracanã Stadium in Rio de Janeiro. This milestone made him one of the most prolific scorers in sports history.

questions.q: Which sport's first world championship was held in India in 1927?
questions.explanation: Hockey's first world championship was held in India in 1927. India has a rich tradition in field hockey and has won multiple Olympic medals in the sport, establishing it as a major hockey nation.
